ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for pork cutlet with egg sandwich set. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Sandwiches) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for pork cutlet egg sandwich set in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.

serving.description: 1 set (sandwich + beverage side; sandwich portion estimated)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 280 g, representing a pork cutlet and egg sandwich set as typically served at a Singaporean kopitiam or Toast Box outlet, comprising two slices of toasted bread with a breaded pork cutlet and a fried or scrambled egg.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite: Breaded pork cutlet (SR Legacy #17166) and egg sandwich components; adapted for Singaporean kopitiam serving style
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods for this specific Toast Box menu item. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for breaded fried pork cutlet (approximately 55% of sandwich weight) and fried egg (approximately 20%), with white toast bread (approximately 25%), weighted to a per-100g basis. Values are cross-checked against NCC Food and Nutrient Database generic sandwich category anchors. Confidence is medium due to estimation from components.

gi_source.title: White bread GI reference and breaded meat sandwich analogous estimate — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pork cutlet with egg sandwich set. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the dominant carbohydrate source being white toast bread (GI ~70–75 per University of Sydney GI Database) moderated by the substantial protein and fat content from the breaded pork cutlet and egg, which are known to lower the effective glycemic response of a mixed meal. Analogous mixed meat-and-bread sandwich GI values in the range 45–60 are reported in peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 57.4) / 100 ≈ 31.6, where carbohydrate per 280 g serving = 20.5 × 2.8 = 57.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1176 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this sandwich set due to its high sodium content, which can contribute significantly to daily sodium intake; choosing lower-sodium bread and reducing added condiments such as mayonnaise or soy-based sauces is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~55) but high glycemic load per serving (~31.6) due to substantial carbohydrate portion from white toast bread
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this set meal as, despite its low glycemic index, the large serving size and carbohydrate content from white toast bread result in a high glycemic load; substituting wholegrain bread and strictly monitoring portion size can help moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~3.4 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium from deep-frying and condiments
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish in moderation given its saturated fat and sodium content from the breaded deep-fried pork cutlet; opting for a smaller portion and avoiding high-fat condiments can reduce cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Pork Cutlet with Egg Sandwich Set is a popular Singaporean kopitiam-style meal served at outlets such as Toast Box. It consists of a breaded and deep-fried pork loin cutlet paired with a fried or scrambled egg, assembled between two slices of toasted white bread, and typically served as a set with a hot beverage such as kopi (local coffee) or teh (local tea). The pork cutlet is seasoned, coated in breadcrumbs, and fried until golden and crispy, yielding a crunchy exterior with a juicy, tender interior. The egg adds richness and protein, while the toasted bread provides a soft yet slightly crisp base. The overall flavour profile is savoury, umami-forward, and mildly rich, with the egg and pork complementing each other. Nutritionally, the set is a moderate-to-high calorie meal (~694 kcal per serving) with significant protein (~37.8 g), moderate fat (~35.8 g), and a substantial carbohydrate load (~57.4 g) primarily from white bread. Sodium content is elevated (~1176 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic load is high per serving due to the white bread base, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is not halal as it contains pork. It is a culturally significant breakfast or brunch item in Singapore's kopitiam food culture, reflecting the local tradition of pairing Western-style toast with local coffee-shop beverages.

confidence_reason: No exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional database for this specific Toast Box menu item. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted calculation using USDA SR Legacy data for breaded fried pork cutlet and fried egg, cross-referenced with NCC generic sandwich anchors. GI is an analogous estimate based on white bread GI from the University of Sydney GI Database moderated by protein and fat content. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved databases and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for breaded fried pork cutlet and fried egg, combined with white toast bread in approximate proportions (55% pork cutlet, 20% egg, 25% bread by weight), normalised to per-100g basis. No exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry was found for this dish. 2. Serving size of 280 g is an estimate for the sandwich portion of the set; the beverage component (kopi or teh) is excluded from nutrient calculations as it is a separate item. 3. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI 55 is correctly categorized as 'low'. 4. The dish contains pork and is therefore not halal. 5. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are not directly measured for this specific product. 6. All -1 sentinel values have been replaced with estimated numeric values from USDA SR Legacy component data; no -1 values remain in per_100g. 7. Allergens include gluten (bread, breadcrumbs), egg, soy (possible in seasoning or bread), and milk (possible in bread or butter spread); individuals with these allergies should exercise caution.
